engineIntelligence.h2: Subaru Levorg Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Subaru Levorg in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Subaru Levorg FB16DIT engine - petrol 1.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: Carbon buildup intake valves - direct injection causes deposits on valve backs at 40k-70k miles.
Turbo wastegate rattle - actuator wear causes boost inconsistency on higher-mileage units.
Oil consumption - piston ring wear increases oil burn beyond 60k miles on some examples.

cards.altText: Subaru Levorg FB20DIT engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Carbon buildup intake valves - DIT system deposits accumulate on valve backs requiring cleaning.
Turbocharger bearing wear - oil starvation or delayed changes cause shaft play above 70k miles.
CVT compatibility - Lineartronic integration requires precise engine mounting and ECU matching.

cards.altText: Subaru Levorg CB18DIT engine - petrol 1.8L replacement UK
knownFailures: Carbon buildup intake valves - updated DIT system still vulnerable to valve deposits on short journeys.
Turbo actuator calibration - electronic wastegate requires specialist reset after replacement.
Cooling system sensitivity - facelift models demand precise thermostat operation to prevent overheating.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,800-£4,500 (supply only) Estimated from comparable CB18DIT units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: Subaru Levorg Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Subaru Levorg across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: The FB16DIT launched the Levorg with efficient 1.6L turbo power, delivering 170hp while meeting UK tax bands - but DIT direct injection introduced carbon buildup concerns.
compatibleVariants: SE, SE Lux, EX (all 1.6 GT trim levels)
commonFailures: Carbon buildup on intake valves - documented across all DIT applications, typically 40k-70k miles
Turbo wastegate actuator wear - causes boost inconsistency and rattle on higher-mileage units
Oil consumption from piston ring wear - reported on examples above 60k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for FB16DIT Subaru Levorg engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The FB20DIT brought performance to the Levorg GT range with 268hp output, demanding meticulous maintenance but rewarding enthusiasts with genuine sports tourer capability.
compatibleVariants: GT, GT Sport, STI Sport (all 2.0 performance trim levels)
commonFailures: Carbon buildup on intake valves - DIT system deposits require periodic walnut blasting or professional cleaning
Turbocharger bearing wear - oil starvation or extended service intervals cause shaft play above 70k miles
CVT Lineartronic compatibility - replacement engines require precise ECU and mounting verification
entries.cta: Get quotes for FB20DIT Subaru Levorg engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: The CB18DIT replaced the FB16DIT in the 2020 facelift, offering refined efficiency and updated emissions compliance while retaining the Levorg's turbo boxer character.
compatibleVariants: GT 1.8 Facelift, GT Sport 1.8 Facelift (VN generation models)
commonFailures: Carbon buildup on intake valves - updated DIT strategy reduces but does not eliminate valve deposit risk
Electronic wastegate calibration - requires specialist reset procedures after turbo or engine replacement
Cooling system sensitivity - facelift models demand precise thermostat operation to prevent overheating
entries.cta: Get quotes for CB18DIT Subaru Levorg engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Subaru Levorg Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Subaru Levorg's DIT turbo boxer engines deliver distinctive performance but face model-specific challenges. Carbon buildup on intake valves dominates replacement enquiries across all FB/CB-series engines, while turbo wastegate wear and oil consumption affect higher-mileage examples. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Carbon Buildup on Intake Valves - Why Subaru Levorg DIT Engines Struggle
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Levorg SE, GT, EX, STI Sport (2014-2024, FB16DIT/FB20DIT/CB18DIT)
problems.rootCause: Direct injection systems spray fuel directly into the combustion chamber, bypassing the intake valves - this prevents the natural cleaning effect of fuel washing over valve backs, allowing carbon deposits to accumulate.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Walnut blasting or chemical cleaning of intake valves without engine removal
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only before severe deposits cause misfires; not a fix for damaged valves

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, professionally clean valves, replace gaskets, reseal
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if block and pistons are undamaged; requires specialist assessment

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt FB/CB engine with professionally cleaned intake ports, bench-tested, warranty included
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for confirmed severe carbon damage; addresses root cause comprehensively

problems.recommendation: If your Levorg shows early symptoms like rough idle or slight power loss but runs well, a specialist intake cleaning can extend life cost-effectively. For confirmed severe carbon damage with misfires or compression loss, replacement with a rebuilt unit featuring professionally cleaned intake ports offers better long-term value than repeated cleaning attempts.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt FB16DIT engine prices for your Subaru Levorg

problems.h4: Turbocharger Wastegate Failure - Why Levorg Boost Pressure Drops
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Levorg GT, GT Sport, STI Sport (2014-2024, FB20DIT/CB18DIT)
problems.rootCause: Turbo wastegate actuators experience wear from heat cycling and boost pressure loads; electronic actuators on facelift models can suffer calibration drift or motor failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace wastegate actuator, reset ECU adaptations, test boost curve
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if turbo housing and bearings are undamaged; not a fix for seized turbos

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, replace bearings/seals/actuator, balance shaft, reinstall
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if engine block is healthy; requires specialist turbo rebuilder

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Turbo
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt FB/CB engine with reconditioned turbo, bench-tested, warranty included
repairOptions.longevity: Best option for catastrophic turbo failure or repeated wastegate issues

problems.recommendation: For Levorg owners noticing boost loss or wastegate rattle, a specialist turbo assessment can determine if actuator replacement is viable. If the turbo has failed catastrophically or the engine has suffered oil starvation, replacement with a rebuilt FB/CB unit featuring a reconditioned turbo offers the most reliable performance restoration.
problems.cta: Get quotes for FB20DIT Subaru Levorg turbo engine replacement

problems.h4: Oil Consumption - Why Early FB-Series Levorg Engines Burn Oil
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Levorg SE, GT, EX (2014-2018, FB16DIT/FB20DIT pre-update)
problems.rootCause: Early FB-series piston ring specification allowed excessive oil passage into the combustion chamber; Subaru revised rings post-2018 but earlier engines remain vulnerable.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace PCV valve, valve cover gaskets, monitor consumption
repairOptions.longevity: Only addresses symptoms; does not fix ring-related oil burn

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove engine, replace pistons/rings with updated spec, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block is undamaged; labour-intensive, requires specialist

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt FB16DIT/FB20DIT with updated rings, bench-tested, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for confirmed high oil consumption; addresses root cause

problems.recommendation: If your pre-2018 Levorg uses over 1 litre per 1,000 miles, monitor closely and top up regularly to avoid damage. For confirmed high consumption, replacement with a rebuilt FB engine featuring the revised piston ring specification offers the most reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt FB20DIT engine prices for your Subaru Levorg

problems.h4: CVT Lineartronic Compatibility - When Drivetrain Issues Mimic Engine Failure
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Levorg all variants (2014-2024, FB16DIT/FB20DIT/CB18DIT with Lineartronic)
problems.rootCause: CVT belt/chain wear, valve body solenoid failure, or fluid degradation can cause shuddering, loss of drive, or warning lights that owners may mistake for engine problems.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Drain/refill with genuine Subaru CVT fluid, reset adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative only; does not fix mechanical wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove transmission, replace valve body assembly, recalibrate
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if internal belt/chain is undamaged; requires specialist diagnostics

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine + CVT Package
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with reconditioned CVT, full warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option for combined engine/transmission concerns or high-mileage vehicles

problems.recommendation: If your Lineartronic Levorg shudders or loses drive but the engine runs smoothly, request a CVT-specific diagnostic before assuming engine failure. For confirmed transmission issues on high-mileage examples, a combined engine/CVT replacement package from a specialist often delivers better value than separate repairs.
problems.cta: Compare Subaru Levorg drivetrain replacement prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Subaru Levorg
finalCta.paragraph: Whether your Levorg suffers carbon buildup, turbo wastegate wear, or oil consumption, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Subaru Levorg specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components designed to address the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.

Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. engineYears.h2: Subaru Levorg Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Subaru Levorg's UK production history, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: VM Generation Launch: FB16DIT & FB20DIT Debut
years.description: The Levorg launched with two turbo boxer options - the tax-efficient 1.6L FB16DIT and performance-oriented 2.0L FB20DIT - establishing the model's sports tourer credentials.
keyChanges: Levorg introduced to UK market with standard AWD and Lineartronic CVT
FB16DIT (1.6L turbo) and FB20DIT (2.0L turbo) engines available from launch
SE, SE Lux, GT, GT Sport trim levels differentiated by equipment, not engine
mainEngines: FB16DIT - 1.6L turbo petrol (SE / SE Lux / EX)
FB20DIT - 2.0L turbo petrol (GT / GT Sport / STI Sport)

faq.h2: Subaru Levorg Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Subaru Levorg engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt Subaru Levorg engine prices range from £1,400 for a 1.6L FB16DIT to £4,500 for a 1.8L CB18DIT facelift, supply only. Supply & fit adds £600-£1,400 depending on workshop location.
keyPoints: FB16DIT 1.6L rebuilt: £1,400-£3,800 (most common replacement)
FB20DIT 2.0L rebuilt: £1,600-£4,200 (performance variant)
CB18DIT 1.8L rebuilt: £1,800-£4,500 (facelift model)
Supply & fit labour typically adds £600-£1,400 to engine price
items.cta: Compare Subaru Levorg engine replacement prices today

items.question: Is the Subaru Levorg FB16DIT engine reliable?
items.answer: The FB16DIT delivers efficient turbo power but faces well-documented carbon buildup on intake valves between 40k-70k miles on Levorg models. Post-failure, rebuilt units with professionally cleaned intake ports offer improved longevity.
keyPoints: Carbon buildup on intake valves is the FB16DIT's dominant failure mode in Levorg application
Upgraded piston rings in post-2018 units reduce oil consumption concerns
Regular DIT-specific maintenance helps delay carbon deposit accumulation
Rebuilt engines with cleaned intake ports address the root design limitation
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt FB16DIT Subaru Levorg engines

items.question: What causes carbon buildup on Subaru Levorg DIT engines?
items.answer: Direct injection systems spray fuel directly into the combustion chamber, bypassing the intake valves - this prevents the natural cleaning effect of fuel washing over valve backs, allowing carbon deposits to accumulate on Subaru Levorg DIT engines.
keyPoints: DIT direct injection design inherently vulnerable to valve deposit accumulation
Symptoms include rough idle, power loss, and increased fuel consumption
Rebuilt engines with professionally cleaned intake ports address the root design limitation
Regular walnut blasting or chemical cleaning helps manage deposits pre-failure
items.cta: Find Subaru Levorg carbon buildup replacement solutions

items.question: Are Subaru Levorg turbo engines worth replacing?
items.answer: FB20DIT and CB18DIT turbo Levorg engines face carbon buildup and turbo wastegate challenges on higher-mileage UK examples; replacement makes sense for low-mileage vehicles with strong service history, but budget for potential turbo system upgrades.
keyPoints: Turbo wastegate actuator wear dominates FB20DIT/CB18DIT failure enquiries above 60k miles
Carbon buildup on intake valves requires periodic professional cleaning on all DIT engines
Rebuilt turbo units can include updated wastegate components for improved reliability
Consider total vehicle value versus replacement cost before proceeding
items.cta: Compare Subaru Levorg turbo engine replacement quotes

items.question: Can I fit a Subaru Levorg engine myself?
items.answer: Boxer engine layout, DIT turbo complexity and Lineartronic CVT integration make DIY Subaru Levorg engine replacement inadvisable for most owners; specialist fitting ensures correct torque specs, fluid procedures and warranty validity.
keyPoints: Boxer engine access requires specific tools and Subaru experience
DIT fuel system and turbo calibration demand specialist knowledge
Most rebuilt engine warranties require specialist fitment for validation
Supply & fit service typically adds £600-£1,400 but protects your investment
items.cta: Find Subaru Levorg engine supply & fit specialists near you

items.question: What's the difference between reconditioned and rebuilt Subaru Levorg engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned Subaru Levorg engines address specific known failures like carbon buildup or turbo wear; rebuilt units undergo full disassembly with upgraded components throughout, offering longer warranty coverage and comprehensive failure prevention.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: targeted repair of documented failure points, lower cost
Rebuilt: full teardown, machining, upgraded parts, 12-month warranty standard
Rebuilt recommended for confirmed catastrophic failures or high-mileage vehicles
Both options available supply-only or with specialist fitment nationwide
items.cta: Compare reconditioned vs rebuilt Subaru Levorg engine options

items.question: How long does a Subaru Levorg engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only Subaru Levorg engines typically dispatch within 2-5 working days; specialist fitment adds 1-3 days depending on workshop availability, with most customers back on the road within one week of order confirmation.
keyPoints: Engine sourcing: 1-3 days for common codes (FB16DIT, FB20DIT), longer for CB18DIT
Delivery: Nationwide courier typically 1-2 days from dispatch
Fitment: 1-2 days for experienced Subaru specialists
Post-install testing and fluid top-up included in professional service
items.cta: Check Subaru Levorg engine delivery and fitment timelines

items.question: Will any Subaru engine fit my Levorg?
items.answer: FB-series engines generally interchange within Levorg generations, but CB18DIT facelift units require specific ECU and wiring compatibility checks; always verify engine code match before purchasing a replacement Subaru Levorg unit.
keyPoints: FB16DIT/FB20DIT swap possible within VM generation with ECU adaptation
CB18DIT requires matching VN generation wiring harness and ECU programming
DIT fuel system compatibility critical for plug-and-play fitment
Use your registration lookup to confirm exact compatible engine codes
items.cta: Verify compatible engines for your Subaru Levorg registration

items.question: Is my Subaru Levorg worth repairing after engine failure?
items.answer: For Levorgs valued above £4,000 with good service history, rebuilt engine replacement typically offers better long-term value than part-exchange; below this threshold, compare replacement cost against vehicle market value.
keyPoints: Factor in vehicle age, mileage, service history and overall condition
Rebuilt engines with 12-month warranty restore reliability for continued ownership
Consider selling non-running Levorg to specialist buyer if repair uneconomical
EngineMarket comparison tool helps assess realistic replacement costs instantly
items.cta: Get tailored quotes for your Subaru Levorg engine replacement decision

items.question: What warranty comes with Subaru Levorg replacement engines?
items.answer: Every Subaru Levorg engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour for manufacturing defects; terms vary by supplier so confirm exact coverage before purchase.
keyPoints: Standard warranty: 12 months unlimited mileage on rebuilt/reconditioned units
Used engines typically carry shorter 3-6 month warranty coverage
Warranty validity often requires specialist fitment and service records
Keep all documentation for potential warranty claims post-installation
